metadata.description: Comparative validation of Gamma vs Gaussian HRF shapes and properties

semantic_description.purpose: Validate and compare Gamma and Gaussian HRF implementations with their distinct mathematical properties
semantic_description.algorithm:
Two fundamental HRF shapes based on probability distributions:
**Gamma HRF:** h_gamma(t) = dgamma(t, shape, rate) for t >= 0 - Uses gamma probability density function - Default: shape=6, rate=1 - Peak at (shape-1)/rate for shape > 1 - Asymmetric with longer right tail - Zero for t < 0
**Gaussian HRF:** h_gaussian(t) = dnorm(t, mean, sd) for all t - Uses normal probability density function - Default: mean=6, sd=2 - Peak at mean - Symmetric around mean - Non-zero for all t (but negligible for |t-mean| >> sd)
**Key Differences:** 1. Support: Gamma on [0,∞), Gaussian on (-∞,∞) 2. Symmetry: Gamma asymmetric, Gaussian symmetric 3. Tail behavior: Gamma heavier right tail, Gaussian equal tails 4. Peak timing: Gamma at (shape-1)/rate, Gaussian at mean 5. Biological realism: Gamma more realistic (causal, t≥0)
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**Gamma Properties (shape=6, rate=1):** - Peak at t = 5 seconds - Mode = (shape-1)/rate = 5 - Mean = shape/rate = 6 - Variance = shape/rate² = 6 - Right-skewed distribution - h(0) = 0, monotonic rise to peak, then decay
**Gaussian Properties (mean=6, sd=2):** - Peak at t = 6 seconds - Mode = Mean = 6 - Variance = sd² = 4 - Symmetric around mean - h(0) ≈ 0.0001 (small but non-zero) - Bell-shaped curve
**Comparative Properties:** - Similar peak timing with default parameters - Gamma peak slightly earlier (5s vs 6s) - Gamma more realistic causality (zero before onset) - Different tail behaviors affect convolution results
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Gamma: t < 0 should return exactly 0 - Gaussian: Very negative t should return values near 0 - Shape parameter effect on gamma peak timing - SD parameter effect on gaussian width - Rate vs shape parameter interactions

input.name: time_vector
input.description: Time points from 0 to 25 seconds with 0.1 second intervals

input.name: extended_time
input.description: Extended time including negative values for gaussian comparison

input.name: gamma_custom
input.description: Custom gamma parameters for shape comparison

input.name: gaussian_custom
input.description: Custom gaussian parameters for width comparison

check.location: abs(hrf_gaussian(4, mean=6, sd=2) - hrf_gaussian(8, mean=6, sd=2))
abs(hrf_gamma(3, shape=6, rate=1) - hrf_gamma(7, shape=6, rate=1))
hrf_gamma(15, shape=6, rate=1) / hrf_gaussian(15, mean=6, sd=2)

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Functions: hrf_gamma(t, shape=6, rate=1) and hrf_gaussian(t, mean=6, sd=2) # Location: R/hrf-functions.R
# Test time vectors time_vec <- seq(0, 25, by = 0.1) extended_time <- seq(-5, 25, by = 0.1)
# Default parameter tests gamma_default <- hrf_gamma(time_vec, shape = 6, rate = 1) gaussian_default <- hrf_gaussian(time_vec, mean = 6, sd = 2)
# Custom parameter tests gamma_custom <- hrf_gamma(time_vec, shape = 8, rate = 1.5) gaussian_custom <- hrf_gaussian(time_vec, mean = 5, sd = 1.5)
# Specific value tests gamma_values <- hrf_gamma(c(0, 5, 6, 10, 15), shape = 6, rate = 1) gaussian_values <- hrf_gaussian(c(0, 4, 6, 8, 15), mean = 6, sd = 2)
# Edge case tests gamma_negative <- hrf_gamma(c(-5, -1, -0.1), shape = 6, rate = 1) gaussian_extended <- hrf_gaussian(extended_time, mean = 6, sd = 2)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation following semantic specification
import numpy as np from scipy import stats
def hrf_gamma(t, shape=6, rate=1): """ Gamma HRF using gamma probability density function.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array) shape: Shape parameter (default: 6) rate: Rate parameter (default: 1)
Returns: Gamma HRF values at time points t """ t = np.asarray(t, dtype=float) # scipy.stats.gamma uses scale = 1/rate scale = 1.0 / rate return stats.gamma.pdf(t, a=shape, scale=scale)
def hrf_gaussian(t, mean=6, sd=2): """ Gaussian HRF using normal probability density function.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array) mean: Mean parameter (default: 6) sd: Standard deviation parameter (default: 2)
Returns: Gaussian HRF values at time points t """ t = np.asarray(t, dtype=float) return stats.norm.pdf(t, loc=mean, scale=sd)
